message.text:
Artifact: Dockerfile
Type: dockerfile
Vulnerability DS-0001
Severity: MEDIUM
Message: Specify a tag in the 'FROM' statement for image 'ubuntu'
Link: [DS-0001](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0001)

Artifact: Dockerfile
Type: dockerfile
Vulnerability DS-0002
Severity: HIGH
Message: Last USER command in Dockerfile should not be 'root'
Link: [DS-0002](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0002)

Artifact: Dockerfile
Type: dockerfile
Vulnerability DS-0026
Severity: LOW
Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile
Link: [DS-0026](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0026)

fullDescription.text: When using a 'FROM' statement you should use a specific tag to avoid uncontrolled behavior when the image is updated.

help.markdown:
**Misconfiguration DS-0001**
| Type | Severity | Check | Message | Link |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|Dockerfile Security Check|MEDIUM|':latest' tag used|Specify a tag in the 'FROM' statement for image 'ubuntu'|[DS-0001](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0001)|

When using a 'FROM' statement you should use a specific tag to avoid uncontrolled behavior when the image is updated.
help.text:
Misconfiguration DS-0001
Type: Dockerfile Security Check
Severity: MEDIUM
Check: ':latest' tag used
Message: Specify a tag in the 'FROM' statement for image 'ubuntu'
Link: [DS-0001](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0001)
When using a 'FROM' statement you should use a specific tag to avoid uncontrolled behavior when the image is updated.

fullDescription.text: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.

help.markdown:
**Misconfiguration DS-0002**
| Type | Severity | Check | Message | Link |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|Dockerfile Security Check|HIGH|Image user should not be 'root'|Last USER command in Dockerfile should not be 'root'|[DS-0002](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0002)|

Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
help.text:
Misconfiguration DS-0002
Type: Dockerfile Security Check
Severity: HIGH
Check: Image user should not be 'root'
Message: Last USER command in Dockerfile should not be 'root'
Link: [DS-0002](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0002)
Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.

fullDescription.text: You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.

help.markdown:
**Misconfiguration DS-0026**
| Type | Severity | Check | Message | Link |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|Dockerfile Security Check|LOW|No HEALTHCHECK defined|Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile|[DS-0026](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0026)|

You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
help.text:
Misconfiguration DS-0026
Type: Dockerfile Security Check
Severity: LOW
Check: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Message: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your Dockerfile
Link: [DS-0026](https://avd.aquasec.com/misconfig/ds-0026)
You should add HEALTHCHECK instruction in your docker container images to perform the health check on running containers.
